title: convolvulus arvensis
intro: Convolvulus arvensis, also known as field bindweed or creeping Jenny, is a perennial plant in the morning glory family that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Convolvulus arvensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Convolvulus arvensis can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or topical ointment, and it has been used in folk remedies to treat a range of ailments.
